name: Baïse Cycle Route (V82): Biran Loop
name_original: Biran et Ordan-Larroque – boucle dans la vallée de la Baïse
description: This moderate 20.0-mile (32.2 km) touring cycling loop in the Baïse Valley offers scenic views of orchards, vineyards, and rolling hills.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Jegun traverse a landscape characterized by rolling, hilly countryside in the Gers department of France. The region features varied terrain, including roads through Armagnac vineyards and peaceful forests. Cyclists can expect routes that connect historic villages and offer views of the surrounding agricultural land. Best touring cycling routes around Jegun The most popular touring cycling route is Lavardens Village – Lavardens Castle loop from Peyrusse-Massas, a 14.6 miles (23.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 36 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain around the historic Lavardens Castle. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Baïse Cycle Route (V82): Biran Loop, a moderate 20.0 miles (32.2 km) path. This route explores the countryside around Biran, featuring scenic views and local points of interest. Local touring cyclists also love the Lavardens Castle – Lavardens Village loop from Castéra-Verduzan, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail leading through the Gers countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Jegun is defined by hilly countryside, Armagnac vineyards, and historic villages. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Jegun are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 11,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Jegun's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Jegun?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes around Jegun, offering a wide range of options for exploring the Gers department. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Jegun?
A: The terrain around Jegun is characterized by its &lt;em&gt;rolling, hilly countryside&lt;/em&gt;, providing varied cycling experiences. You'll traverse roads through picturesque Armagnac vineyards and peaceful forests, with some climbs to conquer. The landscape often offers scenic views, and on some routes, you might even catch glimpses of the Pyrenean mountains in the background.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed ride?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for an easier pace. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40933056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavardens Castle – Lavardens Village loop from Mérens&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, covering about 5.6 km. While the region is generally hilly, this route provides a more accessible experience.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near charming historical sites. You can explore the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1473257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavardens Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a 17th-century marvel offering stunning views. Other notable places include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5722439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cézan Fortified Village&lt;/a&gt;, the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vic-Fezensac&lt;/a&gt;, and the village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biran&lt;/a&gt;, known for its church and watchtower. The wider Gers region also boasts UNESCO World Heritage sites like Auch Cathedral and Flaran Abbey.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes around Jegun?
A: While many routes are moderate in length, you can find longer options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24838733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavardens Castle – Lavardens Village loop from Castéra-Verduzan&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 45.5 km trail that takes nearly 3 hours to complete, offering an extended exploration of the Gers countryside.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Jegun?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the picturesque views of vineyards and forests, and the opportunity to discover historic villages and castles. The blend of challenging and leisurely routes also caters to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Jegun area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Jegun are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10609582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavardens Village – Lavardens Castle loop from Peyrusse-Massas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021109264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baïse Cycle Route (V82): Biran Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic round trips.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Jegun?
A: The Gers region, including Jegun, is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. The mild weather during these seasons makes for comfortable rides through the rolling countryside and vineyards. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred. Winter cycling is possible but can be cooler and less predictable.

Q: Are there options for more challenging or advanced touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The hilly nature of the Gers department provides ample opportunities for more advanced cyclists. Over half of the routes (34 out of 63) are classified as difficult. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24838733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavardens Castle – Lavardens Village loop from Castéra-Verduzan&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult 45.5 km route with significant elevation changes, perfect for those seeking a challenge.

Q: Can I find routes that offer scenic viewpoints of the region?
A: Yes, the rolling countryside around Jegun is inherently scenic. Many routes offer panoramic views of the Armagnac vineyards, lush forests, and historic villages. The routes around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1473257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavardens Castle&lt;/a&gt; are particularly noted for their stunning vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any specific routes that are particularly good for exploring the Armagnac vineyards?
A: Many routes in the Jegun area will take you through the heart of the Armagnac region. The general landscape is characterized by these vineyards, so most touring cycling routes will offer views and passages through them. For a dedicated experience, look for routes that specifically mention traversing vineyard areas, such as those around Lavardens and Biran, which are nestled within this renowned wine-producing region.

Q: What are the options for parking near the touring cycling routes in Jegun?
A: While specific parking information for each route start point isn't always detailed, Jegun and the surrounding villages typically offer public parking areas. When planning your tour, it's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for any user-provided parking tips or to look for designated parking in the nearest village or town.
tips: Small, very quiet road, on the Baïse valley cycle route.
Small town in the heart of Gascony, between the Baïse and Auloue rivers and which stretches on a ridge along a single street. There is a small climbing site at the top of the village for amateurs.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The Biran et Ordan-Larroque loop is approximately 32.2 kilometers (20 miles) long. While an experienced cyclist might complete it in around 2 hours, we recommend allowing 3-4 hours to comfortably enjoy the scenic views and manage the moderate climbs without rushing.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for the Biran et Ordan-Larroque cycling loop, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a loop, so you can start from various points. While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, you can typically find parking in or near the villages of Biran or Ordan-Larroque. It's advisable to check local village parking options upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bicycles. However, be prepared for some sections that might be unpaved or less maintained. The Baïse Valley is characterized by rolling hills, so you will encounter both ascents and descents throughout the loop.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The Biran et Ordan-Larroque loop is categorized as 'moderate' with an elevation gain of about 410 meters. While it requires 'good physical condition,' it's also described as 'suitable for all levels,' implying that regular cyclists with a reasonable level of fitness will find it manageable. Families with younger children or beginners might find the climbs challenging, but it's not overly technical.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Biran et Ordan-Larroque loop?
faqs.answer: The Baïse Valley is beautiful throughout the warmer months. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with fewer crowds. Summer (June-August) can be warm, so an early start is recommended if cycling during these months.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and views can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque landscapes typical of the 'happy valley' region. The route offers bucolic views of orchards, vineyards, grain fields, and forests. The area is known for its gentle hills and fertile valleys, and on clear days, distant views of the Pyrenees might be visible from certain vantage points.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks on the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is known for its natural beauty, you'll pass through the settlement of Biran. Another notable feature in the vicinity is Chemin de Herrebouc. The wider Baïse Valley is rich in heritage, with historical villages and sites, adding to the cultural backdrop of your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Biran et Ordan-Larroque loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a touring bicycle route primarily on paved and public roads, dogs are generally allowed if they are safely secured in a bicycle trailer or basket. However, always ensure your dog is comfortable and safe for the duration of the ride, and be mindful of local regulations in any villages you pass through.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Biran et Ordan-Larroque loop. It utilizes public roads and paths, making it freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there places to find refreshments or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: The villages of Biran and Ordan-Larroque, as well as other settlements in the wider Baïse Valley, may offer cafes, small shops, and accommodation options. It's a good idea to check availability in advance, especially if you plan to stop for a meal or stay overnight.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other cycling trails?
faqs.answer:
Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails. Notably, about 20% of the loop follows the

Véloroute de la Baïse

. Smaller portions also intersect with the

Chemin de Ronde

and

Chemin de la Fontaine de Couhelongue

.

faqs.question: Given the moderate difficulty, are there any tips for managing the climbs on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features rolling hills with a total elevation gain of over 400 meters. To manage the climbs effectively, consider pacing yourself, using appropriate gearing, and taking short breaks to enjoy the views. Starting earlier in the day can also help avoid the warmest temperatures, especially during summer months.
